focus: Deep-research mechanics to transfer into deep-review
summary: Research mechanics should transfer as candidate-generation pressure: narrow candidate focus, structured hypothesis/invariant/producer/consumer/negative-test rows, reducer-owned ruled-out directions, evidence enumeration for clean paths, and candidate-class convergence gates before severity adjudication.

label: Question-led research state should become candidate-led review state
summary: Deep-review should keep dimensions, but each dimension needs reducer-owned candidate questions or matrix rows so focus is not only taxonomy coverage.

label: Research delta vocabulary should become review candidate vocabulary
summary: Review should add pre-finding hypothesis, invariant, producer, consumer, negative_test, and candidate_edge records before severity findings.

label: Narrow-focus behavior should be added inside each review dimension
summary: Deep-review should select a dimension and then exhaust one narrow candidate cluster before moving on, instead of relying on broad dimension rotation.

label: Research negative knowledge is the missing clean-review proof
summary: Ruled-out bug hypotheses should be reducer-owned in review so clean passes preserve what was searched and why it was eliminated.

label: Evidence enumeration should apply to clean paths, not only active findings
summary: Review needs source/file/producer/consumer/test enumeration for no-finding rows, not only evidence density for active findings.

label: Question entropy should map to candidate-class saturation
summary: Deep-review should block STOP until required candidate classes are found, ruled out with evidence, or explicitly deferred as blockers.

label: Review should adopt research's reducer-owned state discipline
summary: Review agents should write narrative/state/delta artifacts; reducers should update strategy, registry, dashboard, and candidate coverage.

label: Novelty should be measured at candidate-concept level
summary: Severity-weighted newFindingsRatio should remain, but review also needs required candidateNovelty or semanticNovelty for bug-search coverage.

label: Web/source freedom should not transfer into review
summary: The transferable mechanic is structured focus and evidence state, not default external research during code review.

label: Prior packet findings support pre-validation candidate generation
summary: Real artifacts and prompt analysis both point to missing candidate-generation pressure before severity adjudication.
Focused deep-research surfaces more bugs because it persists the search trail, while deep-review often persists the review report after a candidate is noticed.
The first deep-review improvement should be a required candidate-generation matrix for non-trivial code/spec targets, with reducer-owned ruled-out promotion and validator-enforced shape.
Candidate row fields should include dimension, bugClass, invariant, producer, consumer, negativeTestSearched, evidenceRefs, result, linkedFindingId, and ruledOutReason.
Iteration 005 converts the earlier research-delta comparison into a concrete review candidate-record proposal.
Explicit ruled-out candidate records directly address the observed absence of bug-class coverage in structured review details.
Candidate-class saturation is the deeper replacement for shallow dimension coverage.
The transfer should keep deep-review code-only while borrowing research's state and delta shape.

direction: Replace deep-review dimensions with deep-research questions
reason: Rejected. Dimensions remain useful for release-readiness and severity reporting; they need candidate-led subfocus, not removal.
Rejected. P0/P1 adjudication already starts after candidate discovery; the missing depth is pre-adjudication candidate generation.

direction: Make graphEvents mandatory as the only first fix
reason: Rejected as first step. Graph support is valuable, but a validator-enforced candidate matrix is smaller and can work before graph schema expansion.

direction: Allow WebFetch in deep-review to mimic deep-research
reason: Rejected. Review should stay code/spec-local; the transferable mechanics are focus, evidence enumeration, and ruled-out state.
